content: Well, this dinner was really tough. When I finally shared what I thought about the project, everyone seemed to think I’m silly, and I just felt like maybe no one really wants to hear what I have to say. I kept trying to fit in, laughing and agreeing, but it didn’t help much. Honestly, when I saw them laughing at something I didn’t understand, I just stopped trying to contribute because I wanted to seem cool and important. It’s frustrating because I feel like if I show any weakness, people will just think less of me. I guess I’m just afraid of getting left behind or finding out I’m not cut out for these kinds of things. I’d prefer not to go through with anything awkward or embarrassing. So, I just let it slide. It’s like no matter what I try, I’m never quite good enough.

Yeah, this week has been pretty tough for me. When they stopped talking to me lately, I started feeling like maybe they’re bored, or maybe I’m too much of an attention seeker. I kept thinking, “Why don’t they even show up? Why aren’t they showing up?” I ended up feeling really alone and then tried to push them away again, but it only made me feel worse. It’s like no matter how much I try to connect, it always fails to make them happy. So, I just decided to go back to my old ways—pretending to be okay, but really feeling left behind. It’s exhausting to know I’m never going to fill any of that void. I guess I just don’t see why people need me anymore. It makes me wonder if maybe I’m stuck in a rut or not good enough after all.

Yeah, I guess… It’s like when I’m trying to pull off something big and everyone just seems to notice how slow or unclear I am. I keep thinking maybe I’ll mess everything up, making them realize I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff. Then I just end up putting things off because it’s easier to avoid talking about it, so I don’t feel ready to face anyone right now. Honestly, it makes me feel really ashamed inside. I’ve been isolating myself too much lately because I don’t want people to see how bad I’m acting. I just feel like I’m failing, like no one believes in my abilities. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I’m good enough anyway, but I just can’t shake the nagging part. I hope they find out sooner rather than later.

Well, lately, whenever I’m at that party, I start worrying that if I don’t seem too funny or interesting, people will think I’m boring or weird. So, I always try to be very friendly—smiling a lot and agreeing with everyone around me—but then I get really uncomfortable. It’s like when I see someone whispering, and I just feel like I’m being watched or that maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of interaction. I’ve tried to stay quiet because I want to appear confident, but it just makes me feel worse. I guess I just don’t like being the center of attention enough. It’s exhausting sometimes.

Well, last week we had that conversation about next steps, and honestly, I felt really overwhelmed. When they started talking about making changes, I just felt like my voice was fading away, like no matter what I said, it wasn’t going to stick. It’s like I’m stuck in this place where I don’t know how to move forward, and I guess that’s why I’m avoiding it all. I kept thinking, “Why does this have to be like this? There’s nothing left to change.” So instead of saying anything, I just gave up, and then passed up the idea altogether. It makes me feel like maybe I’m really useless here, which is hard because I care deeply about what’s happening between us. I guess this whole feeling of being trapped and helpless just seems to stick around. It’s frustrating and makes me want to drop out of the conversation.

Oh, hey therapist. So, this time at the family reunion… it just felt like everything was going wrong. I kept thinking, “No matter what I say, it won’t change how things are.” I didn’t really speak up because I was scared of being seen as weak or maybe even stupid. Then I just stayed quiet and pretended not to notice because it felt easier not to talk. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m trying to fit in but never truly fitting in. Honestly, I don’t know if I’m ready to face this challenge anymore. It’s exhausting. I guess I’m just saying no more often than I can because I don’t want to upset anyone. I wish they’d let me join some fun activities instead of having all this pressure on me. It’s hard to keep moving forward when I’m so stuck. Thank you for reminding me why I love being someone different. I need you to see through this better. Please, continue with that kind of feedback. I feel like I’m missing something important here.

Yeah, the family gathering was really tough this time. When they started talking about what I wanted to do next, it felt like everyone was expecting me to do something wrong or stupid. I kept thinking, “What if I screw up?” And then, after that moment, I just stayed quiet because I didn’t want to start anything new. Honestly, I felt like I was drowning in this whole thing, and there wasn’t enough room for me to feel better. It’s frustrating because I know I’m capable and that’s why I’m here, but it just feels like no matter what I do, it won’t change everything. I guess it’s hard to trust when you’re constantly being expected and judged. I wish they’d let me talk through it, but sometimes they just shut me down. It’s like they don’t want me to experience any kind of failure or mess-up.

Yeah, well… last time we talked, my partner was really upset when I said something different than they expected. They started complaining because I didn’t ask them what they wanted or if there were some changes I needed to make. I kept agreeing anyway, like, “Okay, whatever,” even though I felt like maybe I wasn’t being enough. Honestly, it made me feel kind of ashamed and scared, like maybe I’m failing at this relationship. I guess it’s easier to stay put than fight back. So, I’ve just suppressed my feelings and tried to be nice again, hoping it’ll stop. But then it just feels like no matter what I say, it’s going to hurt someone else too. It’s frustrating because I want to be strong and honest, but I don’t know how to let go of these little things that seem important. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out what’s going on and why these times come. Please, just let me know exactly where I’m going wrong. I need it fast!

Yeah, the family gathering was really tough for me. When everyone started talking and laughing, I felt like I wasn’t even there. It felt like no one wanted to talk to me because I was just not part of the group. I kept thinking maybe they didn’t want me around or that I’m not important enough. It’s hard to face people when you feel like you’re invisible or irrelevant. I guess I just felt like I was falling apart, feeling like I’m letting go of something I care about. It makes me wonder if I ever truly belong here. I’d rather just be somewhere else, away from all this stuff happening. I don’t know how to stop feeling so bad, though—I’ve tried talking to friends but nothing’s helped. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t escape feeling like I’m not good enough.

Yeah, this past week was really tough. When I looked at my budget, I just felt like my little money stuff were going to fall apart. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t have enough to pay the rent or food? It’s just too much.” I’ve tried to brush things under the rug because I’m afraid others will notice how screwed up I feel. But it’s just overwhelming, and I’m not sure where to start or how to fix it. I feel like no matter what I try, I’ll just fail again. It’s like I’m always falling short, and I don’t want to make it worse. I guess I just don’t trust myself to handle these big life changes. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I need help, but I’m scared of losing my sense of control or being seen as inadequate.

Yeah, so… I got the news about getting the promotion, and honestly, it’s like all my little things fall apart. I keep thinking, “If anyone sees how much trouble I’ve gotten myself into, they’ll just think I’m not cut out for this.” So, I’ve been putting off applying because I’m afraid if I mess up, everyone will find out I’m not capable. It feels like no matter how hard I try, I’m still failing to prove why I deserve this job. I don’t really know how to stop myself—every time I start something new, I end up regretting it. It’s exhausting. I guess I just feel like I’m constantly being judged because I’m not meeting these expectations. I’m sorry if this isn’t coming from a place of weakness, though I hope it’s more than that. I just want to get back on track. But it’s tough. I need help, please. I really appreciate you being here. Please let me know what you’re thinking. I’m scared to say anything too soon.

Yeah, today it felt like everything just clicked in that project. I kept thinking, “If I try to tackle this, it’ll just overwhelm me somehow.” I guess I’m afraid of making mistakes or looking foolish, so I put off doing anything major because I don’t want anyone to notice how incompetent I seem. It’s hard because it feels like no matter what I do, it’s going to fall apart anyway. I keep reminding myself I’m not good enough, and honestly, I just don’t know how to get things done on time. It’s exhausting, and I feel like maybe I’m failing even though I’m already showing signs of struggling. I guess I need someone to tell me that I’m not cut out for this kind of pressure. It’s frustrating because I’m trying really hard to push forward, but I’m always running back into the same wall.

Oh, yeah, the meeting last week… it was really tough. When I met everyone, I felt like I wasn’t even there because I was too quiet. It’s like people were already assuming I wasn’t interested or important enough, so I kept trying to join in, but then I ended up leaving early. Honestly, I just feel really alone and embarrassed. Sometimes I wonder if maybe I’m making things worse by being this quiet and distant. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I want to connect and help, but I just can’t seem to connect at all. I guess I’m just afraid of being seen as stupid or useless. It’s hard to talk to anyone after that unless I’m really trying to impress them. I appreciate it when someone tells me I’m being silly, but honestly, it doesn’t stop me from being myself most of the time.

Well, I guess this week was really rough. My partner kept saying I’m spending too much time at work and not enough time with you. I felt like maybe I’m being such a bad friend because I didn’t even seem to care when they were upset. I’ve been apologizing a lot, but then I ended up canceling some things to try and make them better. It’s hard to deal with all the pressure and expectations I’m putting on myself. Honestly, I just feel like I’m letting them down every time. It’s like I’m constantly failing to live up to what they expect from me. I don’t know why I’m stuck like this. I’d appreciate any advice or perspective you might have. Please, let me know if there’s anything I could change. I’m scared to tell anyone because I don’t want to upset them anymore.

I guess… Well, the day came when I was asked to lead a big project at work, and honestly, I felt like the pressure was too much. I kept thinking, “What if I mess things up? Everyone will see I’m not cut out for this.” So, I said no right away, even though it meant I wouldn’t get to share anything important. It’s exhausting, and I’ve been sleeping less than I usually do because everything feels so overwhelming. Honestly, I just don’t want to admit that I’m failing. It’s frustrating because I know if I didn’t prepare properly, it’d just make them all look down on me. I guess I’m scared that if I show up, people will think less of me or that I’m not good enough. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle of fearing rejection, even when I know it won’t happen.

I’m afraid when I said no to that place, my partner noticed immediately that I wasn’t really interested in what I wanted. They started thinking maybe I don’t actually want anything different, which made me feel… uncomfortable and small. I kept trying to hide my feelings, even if they were obvious. It’s like I was saying yes out of fear, but then I ended up making the same mistake again. Honestly, I just want them to like me better, but instead, I’m stuck in this cycle where I need to be perfect to keep liking them. It’s exhausting, and I don’t know why I can’t stop thinking these things happen all on my own. It’s like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop of needing to please everyone around me. I guess I’m scared that if I ever show some kind of weakness, they’ll just decide I’m not worth loving. So, I’ve chosen to just agree because I don’t want to cause any more pain. But honestly, it’s harder than I thought it would be. I’d love to talk about these feelings with you, but I’m scared they won’t understand how deeply hurtful they’re causing. Please let me know if I’m wrong about these thoughts. I appreciate you taking the chance to see me through this. I really need you to help me figure this out.

I’ve been at this family gathering lately, feeling really bad about myself right after. When someone said something negative about my career choices, I just shut down. It’s like no matter what I do, it doesn’t quite fit perfectly with what they’re expecting. Then I went away early, hoping to leave everything behind, but instead, I felt even more alone and invisible. Honestly, I don’t know why I keep doing this; it’s exhausting and makes me feel worse than I already do. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where no matter what I do, it always seems to fall short of expectations. I guess there’s just something about feeling like I’m not good enough that keeps me feeling so overwhelmed.

I’ve been really struggling lately because my partner keeps suggesting we go somewhere different this year, even though I’m used to going together. I keep thinking, “They won’t want to be with me again,” and then I get overwhelmed when they say no. I’m so anxious and scared that if I let them go, maybe it’ll mean the end of our relationship. I’m constantly trying to convince them to stay, but it just seems easier than pretending to be okay. It’s exhausting, and I feel like I’m making things worse rather than helping. I guess I just don’t trust myself to handle things properly. It’s like I’m afraid they’ll decide to leave me too soon, causing me to lose all hope. I’d appreciate it if you could just tell me what’s happening inside me. I need someone to understand how hard this is for me. Please, help me find ways to connect without feeling abandoned. I’m sorry if this feels wrong, but it’s starting to feel like I’m losing control.

Hey, therapist. I’ve been having some really tough moments lately at work. When new stuff comes in, I get overwhelmed and start doubting myself. I find myself just putting things away or double-checking them multiple times because nothing seems right. It makes me feel like maybe I’m not cut out for this job at all. I keep worrying that if I mess something up, everyone will see me as incompetent. It’s exhausting and makes me want to disappear, but then again, I don’t want to disappoint anyone. Do you think I’m doing enough to improve? Sometimes I feel like I’m failing to measure up to what I believe I deserve. Please help me figure this out, okay? I need you to see where I’m going. I just don’t know why I’m stuck here.

Yeah, the dinner was… kind of hard to attend this time. My parents were really praising my brother’s new business venture, and I just felt like no one else seemed to have anything exciting going on either. It made me feel like maybe I’m truly behind even though I’ve done pretty well myself. I ended up staying late because I didn’t want to come back feeling like I wasn’t important anymore. Honestly, I guess it’s exhausting to keep pushing myself to do better than others. It’s frustrating that I don’t feel like I’m making any difference at all. I guess I’m just not comfortable talking about how much I struggle to keep up. It makes me feel like I’m being judged or not good enough.

Well, um, since I couldn’t go to the party last week, I just felt so… stuck. I kept thinking that if I showed up, everyone would notice immediately and think I’m boring or weird. It’s like no matter what I say or do, it just doesn’t work out right for me. I guess I ended up being quiet because honestly, I didn’t really want to deal with anything other than just feeling embarrassed or left behind. I don’t know, it’s hard to keep going when I feel like I’ve already failed somewhere else. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m being honest, but I’m afraid it’ll just make things worse. I appreciate you reminding me how much I rely on myself sometimes. It’s okay to struggle sometimes—everyone does.

Oh, yeah, that dinner was pretty weird. My parents just started talking about how someone’s friend did something great last night, and then didn’t mention anything about me at all. It made me feel like everyone else was happier than I was, which is so frustrating because I know I’m different and probably even better than they think. I kept thinking maybe I’m just too stupid or not worth anyone’s time, like I’m invisible in this family. After that, I just shut down and didn’t talk much at the end. It’s hard to say exactly why I felt that way, though sometimes it just feels like no one cares about me. I guess it’s okay to stay away sometimes when things get overwhelming—sometimes it helps to step back and let others take care of themselves. I’ll probably replay that dinner again later, and maybe it won’t help me anymore. But honestly, I don’t want to face what I’ve done—I just want to disappear and forget about it.

Yeah, so, after talking to some friends about joining this community group, I just felt like maybe there were too many people around me. I kept thinking, “If I start speaking up, they’ll think I’m weird or boring.” It’s like I’m always putting myself out of sight because no one seems to really want to connect with me. I guess feeling like I’m invisible or awkward makes me feel worse, even though I know I’m probably doing okay socially anyway. It’s frustrating because I want to help others grow and improve, but sometimes I just can’t stop worrying about making mistakes. I guess it’s easier to stay quiet than risk facing anyone if they’re uncomfortable or say something wrong. But then again, it feels like being here won’t make me feel better—like everyone else already knows all my flaws. So, I’ve been hesitant to participate at least until I feel more comfortable and confident enough.

I guess… Well, at the get-together, I just felt really left out. When people started talking without me, I didn’t even try to join in because I was scared I wouldn’t add any value. I kept thinking maybe they’re judging me, or maybe they’re not interested in hearing my ideas. Afterward, I just felt really overwhelmed and kind of depressed. I wish I could tell them something was wrong, but then again, I don’t want to upset anyone. It’s hard to deal with stuff that’s happening so easily. I feel like I’m not making much difference here. I’ve tried to be more active, but it’s harder to keep up when I know I’m going nowhere. It’s exhausting and makes me wonder if I’m not cut out for this kind of life.

I don’t know, therapist. When they talked about this project, I just felt like everything was falling apart. Like I wasn’t good enough to even bother helping them out. I kept thinking, “What if no one notices what I did? Or maybe they’ll say I’m just too much trouble.” Honestly, I didn’t want to start anything right away because then everyone would realize I was just not cut out for this. So, instead, I stayed quiet most of the time and tried to minimize any mention of the project. It’s frustrating because knowing there’s something wrong is like being invisible, but it makes me feel worse than it already does. I guess I just feel trapped and scared that whatever happens won’t be worth trying again. I wish they’d let me try and see how things go, but honestly, I’m scared they’re going to reject me anyway. It’s exhausting to keep hoping things will turn out okay, but it doesn’t help at all. I just don’t know, I guess I’m stuck in this never-ending cycle of fear and doubt.

Hey therapist, I’ve been dealing with this new bill again, and I’m feeling really overwhelmed right now. It’s like nothing I do seems to make any difference—just like when I can’t fix a small thing that happened last time. I keep thinking, “I’m just not cut out for managing money,” and then I end up avoiding even trying because I feel so helpless. It’s like I’m always falling apart, and I don’t want to look like I’m failing. I feel embarrassed and ashamed, but I also feel so helpless. I guess I’m scared that if I try to figure things out, I’ll just embarrass myself again. Do you think maybe it’s time to talk about these feelings and maybe find some help? I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out how to deal with this better. Thank you!

Oh, hey there! So, I know you’ve been talking around this guy at the party. I guess I felt kind of… like, “Nope, don’t even let them get to know you” when they started flirting. I ended up leaving early because I didn’t want to be part of what seemed so obvious to everyone. It just made me feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for relationships. Honestly, I kept thinking, “Why did I have to go through all that? What if they’re just being nice because we’re at the same place?” I guess it’s just harder than I expected when things fall apart between us.

Hey therapist, I’ve been feeling like I’m stuck in this family meeting. It feels like no matter how much I say what I really want, it just doesn’t happen. I don’t know why I keep being pushed to agree when I’m uncomfortable or confused. It’s like everyone thinks I’m a loser when I challenge something important. Sometimes I just shut down and pretend not to notice, but then I end up feeling even worse. I wish I could escape this constant pressure from above. It’s exhausting. Can you see how overwhelming these feelings are? I’m scared of losing face or getting hurt again. I don’t want to go back to the same place every time. What do you think I should do differently? I feel like there’s nothing I can do to change what’s happening. Thank you for reminding me how truly flawed I am.

Yeah, lately, it’s like everything’s going downhill again. When I got the new bank statement, it just made me feel even more stuck. I keep thinking, “I’ll never get this debt down” and that’s enough to make me feel like people don’t really care about me. I’m afraid to talk about it because I’m scared of making things worse or looking stupid. It’s hard to connect with others when I’m constantly feeling overwhelmed and questioning my worth. I guess I just don’t know how to handle these kinds of situations anymore. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle of trying to fix something that doesn’t really need fixing.

I’ve been feeling really anxious lately because whenever I start thinking about what I want or don’t want, I’m afraid it’ll turn out that people aren’t interested in me anymore. It feels like every time I say something different, someone comes along and says no or dismisses me. I keep trying to please everyone around me, but I end up feeling like I’m making things worse instead of better. Sometimes I even shut down completely and just go out only when I’m sure people won’t notice how much I’m doing. I feel so overwhelmed that I never feel comfortable expressing my own needs or asking questions, which makes me feel invisible and maybe even abandoned. It’s like I’m always being pushed away, and I’m scared that if I challenge myself, it’ll just confirm my flaws. I guess I just don’t know how to stop feeling this way. It’s hard to talk to anyone about these feelings because I’m scared of getting criticized or seen as weak. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out why I’m stuck in this cycle of disappointment. Please let me know if there’s anything specific I need to explain more clearly. I’m really struggling to connect with people, and I don’t know if I can ever truly connect again.

Yeah, this week has been really tough so far. When I got this big assignment with overlapping deadlines, I just felt like I couldn’t keep up. I kept thinking, “I’m going to mess this up again,” because no matter how hard I try, I always feel like there’s something missing. I ended up staying late at work almost every night because I didn’t want to show any weakness or let anyone know I wasn’t ready. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like maybe I’m not cut out for what I’m doing here. I guess I just don’t believe I can handle these pressures. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never good enough for them.

Yeah, so lately, I got this big project at work that requires teamwork and tight deadlines. I’ve been thinking a lot about what if I don’t make it? Or maybe people will notice how incompetent I’m becoming. I’ve even stopped working on it entirely—called in sick a few times because I didn’t want to deal with all the stress. It’s like no matter what I do, it just seems too overwhelming and scary. Honestly, I feel pretty overwhelmed, and it makes me question if I really belong here. It’s like I’m afraid of disappointing anyone or showing they’re not up to the job. I don’t know, I guess I just need someone to tell me I’m capable or to help me figure out what I’m supposed to do. It’s frustrating because I want to get this done, but I’m scared of failing again. I guess I’m okay with being invisible, but honestly, it’s hard to stop feeling like I’m not good enough.

I got the notification because… I forgot to open the mail yesterday because there was no one around to check. It’s like I’m trying really hard to make it right, but every time I hit the button, it just seems harder than it already is. Honestly, I don’t know how to start anymore; I just keep putting off doing anything because it makes me feel even worse. Sometimes, when I’m too embarrassed to ask someone, I end up spending all day just trying to cover up my mistakes. It’s exhausting and I just want to leave everything undone. I guess I feel so inadequate because if I mess up financially, everyone will see that I’m failing. It’s like I’m never good enough to handle something alone. I’m scared to show any signs of struggle or let others know what’s wrong, because then they’ll probably think less of me. I’ve tried to talk to my parents, but nothing’s worked out well. It feels like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of fear and embarrassment.

Yeah, this project has been really overwhelming lately. When they gave me the deadline, I just felt like everything was going to fall apart. I kept thinking, “What if no one notices what I’m doing?” And then, when I started working on it, I found myself stuck in a never-ending loop of trying to solve problems instead of getting them solved. It’s exhausting, but honestly, I don’t want anyone to see how bad I’m treating myself. I’ve tried to avoid talking to my coworkers or superiors because I’m afraid they’ll think less of me or judge me for not keeping up. It’s like perfectionism is holding me back from feeling better about myself. I guess I just feel like there’s some part of me that doesn’t belong here anymore.

I’ve been having this really tough evening because when they stop working or respond, it just seems like they’re losing interest in me. I’m scared they’re realizing I’m not good enough or they’ll think I’m a waste. I keep sending messages pretending to care, but it all ends up in my inbox again, and I feel like they’re going to realize I’m annoying them even more. It’s exhausting and makes me want to just disappear—like maybe they’d prefer me gone away. I just don’t know how to stop it. It feels like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop of self-doubt and lack of connection. I guess I’m afraid no matter what, I’m just not cut out for this kind of relationship.

Well, lately, whenever I try to suggest something new or different, I just end up agreeing with what my partner wants, then feeling embarrassed afterward. It’s like, if I say no to something I really don’t care about, it’ll just seem like I’m trying to be annoying or pushy. So, I’ve been avoiding talking about my own ideas because I keep worrying that if I ever show some weakness, it’ll just cause arguments or hurt them. It’s exhausting because it makes me feel like maybe I’m just not good enough. I guess this whole cycle is kind of stuck, where I feel like I need to please everyone so much that I lose my own identity. It’s hard to break through those waves. I appreciate it when people just let me alone, but honestly, sometimes I feel like I’m losing control somehow. It’s like I’m trapped in this never-ending loop of wanting more and hoping against hope.

Yeah, the family gathering was really hard for me. When everyone started paying more attention to other relatives, it just felt like no one noticed me at all. I kept thinking, “Why don’t they even show up? Why would they want to talk to me?” It made me feel so isolated and afraid that maybe I’m somehow not good enough or important enough to belong. I ended up staying late last night because I couldn’t bear to start talking anymore. Honestly, it’s like there’s something holding me back, and I’m scared to let anyone see how much I miss them. I guess I just feel trapped in these circles where I’m invisible. It’s exhausting trying to connect with them every day. I wish I could just disappear completely so I can move forward.

Yeah, so last weekend, my brothers were talking about going on vacation, and then came up with this idea that they wanted to go somewhere important for everyone, but I wasn’t invited. I just felt really sad and kind of invisible. It made me feel like maybe they’re just ignoring me because they don’t like me, or maybe they’ve already figured out how much I matter to them. I ended up staying home most of the time, mostly because I didn’t want to be part of something that might be awkward or disappointing. After that, I just started avoiding any big family gatherings or even trying to make friends online, hoping it wouldn’t happen again. It’s hard not to feel like I’m the one who’s left out, especially since I know some of them probably care more than I do. I guess I just don’t know how to deal with things in such a situation. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m afraid to say anything or let them know what’s wrong, which makes me feel even worse.

Yeah, it was kind of like… just a bunch of people laughing at me instead of actually having a plan. I kept thinking, “They’re probably wondering why I’m not really there.” And honestly, it felt so obvious that I didn’t want to say anything because it just made me seem silly or weak. Then I ended up pretending to be interested, but mostly just wanted to fit in somehow, which wasn’t very fun or respectful. It’s like, even though I know deep down I don’t enjoy these kinds of things, it still keeps happening. I guess I just feel like no matter what I do, I’m never going to make a difference. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to stop feeling this way.

Oh, yeah, that was really tough. When they asked me to give that speech, I just froze up. I couldn’t stop talking because it felt like everything else was getting in the way. Then, after I finished, I just felt so embarrassed and helpless, like maybe I’m actually making things worse. I guess I just didn’t want to mess up everyone’s expectations. It’s hard to face that kind of pressure sometimes, especially at a big social event where people expect you to succeed. I don’t know why I always seem to fall flat in these kinds of situations. It’s frustrating, but I’ve learned better not to try again. Maybe it’s better to stay away from those kinds of events altogether.

Yeah, so at the dinner, the conversation was mostly focused on moving in together and getting ready for big changes, like starting a family or going to college. I kept trying to agree, but then something started to happen, and I didn’t really speak much because I felt like maybe they’re judging me or thinking less of me. It’s hard not to feel scared that if I don’t stick to what they say, they’ll think less of me. So, I just nodded along, pretending I understood, but deep inside, I’m just uncomfortable and wondering why they’d even let me in this way. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m always falling apart again. I guess I just don’t know how to break free from these cycles. It’s like when I try to connect with someone, they usually end up leaving anyway. I’m sorry if I’m making things harder for myself.

Yeah, the dinner was really tough for me. When they started talking about my career choices, I felt like I had already made something wrong. It’s hard not to feel embarrassed, especially after everyone was so focused on what I didn’t achieve. Sometimes I just shut down because it feels like no matter how much I try, it’s still going to fall flat. After the meeting, I kept thinking about all the things I said that weren’t quite right, and then I replayed those parts again and again. It’s like I’m trying to fix something that isn’t there, and instead, I end up just making things worse. I guess I feel like I’m failing at life and maybe I’m being too perfect. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know if anyone else can see that flaw in my performance. I’ll probably go back later and just cry myself to sleep because it just doesn’t feel right anymore.

Yeah, it’s really hard lately. When I glance at my bank account, I just feel like everything’s sinking down. I’m afraid I’ll mess up something big and then it’ll come back even worse. It makes me feel like no matter what I do, nothing’s going to change or help me out. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone because it’s too overwhelming, and it just feels harder to face the things that don’t seem to make any difference. I guess I just want to keep things simple and won’t let myself fall further behind. It’s like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop of fear and uncertainty. I wish I could find some kind of relief or ease, but I’m scared it won’t work out. It’s frustrating because money isn’t something I need to worry about, yet it seems to drag me down. I’m sorry if these feelings are coming through, but honestly, I don’t know how else to handle this right now. It feels like no one understands or cares enough about what’s happening to help me.

Well, last week I was given a new project at work, and honestly, when I saw the big red button next to it, I just felt like my head was exploding with pressure. I kept thinking, “What if I can’t handle this? They’ll all notice how little I’m doing,” and then I ended up staying late because I couldn’t stop feeling like I was making a fool of myself. I guess I just wanted to fit everything neatly and look good to everyone else. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how to stop. I’m scared that if I let things go too far, people will think less of me or that I’m not competent enough. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never really good enough enough.

Yeah, the dinner last night was really hard. When they started talking, I just stayed quiet because I didn’t want to start another argument or make them feel like I wasn’t important there. I ended up leaving early because I felt so overwhelmed and I wanted to get away from all this stress. Honestly, it’s like I’m always stuck inside instead of connecting with others. I guess feeling invisible and left out is what keeps me feeling pretty depressed sometimes. It’s frustrating because I know it shouldn’t be like this. I don’t really know why I’m so resistant to being part of these conversations. It feels like I’m just carrying on anyway, but then something happens that makes me lose control. I wish I could talk to someone about how much I miss being included. It’s exhausting, but I can’t seem to shake off the feeling that maybe I’m just failing at life somehow.

Well, lately, whenever we had that family meeting, it felt like no matter what I tried, I just ended up feeling like I was somehow invisible. They kept saying that everyone seemed to care about me, which made me feel like maybe I’m not cut out for the family circle. After that, I just stayed away—so deep and so quiet. It’s frustrating because I know these times are supposed to be special, but I can’t seem to connect with anyone else. It’s like I’m always causing problems, and then when I don’t show up, it’s like everything feels pointless. I feel like I’m failing to live up to what I believe makes me loved. It’s hard to explain exactly why I keep avoiding these gatherings, though I guess I just don’t want to face anything bigger than rejection.

Oh, yeah, that was really hard. When they talked about the holiday plans, I just felt like nothing happened because nobody seemed interested in what I had to say. It’s like no matter how much I tried, it never got noticed. I guess I just didn’t want to make trouble or cause problems. Then I stayed quiet because I wanted to stay safe, so I ended up leaving early. Honestly, I’ve been feeling pretty frustrated lately, like no matter what I try, it just doesn’t work out. It’s exhausting trying to push my ideas onto the table, even though I know they probably wouldn’t change anything. I’m sorry if this feels like a mistake—I just need someone to tell me what’s really going on.

Yeah, last time we were talking about some stuff at the party, it felt kind of like everyone was right there with me. I kept thinking maybe they already knew what I’m going through or that they’d see me as just another part of this world. I tried to join in, but then I just ended up leaving because I didn’t want to seem like I wasn’t important anymore. It’s hard to explain why I feel so isolated sometimes. Sometimes I wonder if maybe I’m just making things harder for myself, like maybe I don’t deserve these connections. It’s like no matter how much I try, I just end up falling apart again.

I’ve been feeling really overwhelmed lately because I’m supposed to lead a big project at work, which means managing all the details right doesn’t just get harder; it makes everything seem easier than it actually is. I’m constantly worrying that if I fail, people will notice how inadequate I am, which usually results in me procrastinating or feeling embarrassed around other coworkers. It’s exhausting to try to explain things clearly without sounding stupid or foolish. Sometimes, I just want to retreat to a quiet corner where there aren’t too many distractions to feel guilty or anxious. I guess I feel like maybe I’m failing at what I’m trying to accomplish, but then I worry it’ll just prove wrong or useless. It’s hard to focus on anything else because I always feel like I’m slipping back into the same old pattern. I’m sorry if this feels like a real experience for someone else—like they’d be able to handle such pressure better. I need help, please. I just need some guidance and understanding about why this happens. Help me figure out what’s happening and how to stop letting myself get caught up in these endless fears and anxieties. I appreciate you thinking this through for me. Please let me know if I’m off track or if I need to change my response. I’m terrified I’m not doing enough or making enough mistakes, and I wish I could just say “No.” But honestly, I just feel like I’m falling apart again. Thank you for your time and patience. I appreciate you. I’ll be there for you, okay? I really need you to help me.

Hey therapist, I’ve been feeling really bad lately because I got an overdraft notice after I forgot to look at my bank. It just seems like no matter how careful I’m being, something keeps happening. I keep thinking that maybe I messed up some simple thing, and then I end up feeling so ashamed and helpless. I don’t know why, but I feel like I’m never going to get back on track with my finances. It’s exhausting and makes me want to just skip work or call someone just to check myself. Honestly, I just feel like I’m letting down everyone around me because I’m afraid they’ll think less of me. It’s frustrating because even though I know I need to fix things, I’m not sure what to do. I guess I’m scared that if I try to figure out where I went wrong, people will find out I’m incompetent. I appreciate it when you ask me these questions, but I just don’t feel ready to talk about it anymore. Please let me know what I’m missing, okay? I’m sorry if this isn’t very helpful, but I need some clarity. Thank you!

Yeah, the family meeting was really stressful. When they asked me to talk about the ethics thing, I just froze. I kept thinking, “What if someone notices how much trouble I’m causing? Then they’ll just say I’m a failure.” I didn’t want to mess things up, so I stayed quiet and replayed everything over and over in my head. It’s like, no matter how hard I try, I still feel like I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff. I guess I just don’t know why I keep feeling so tied up when it comes to being ethical. It makes me wonder if there’s something fundamentally flawed about me that prevents me from doing the right thing.

Yeah, the family gathering was really hard for me. When they started talking about planning Christmas trips, it felt like everyone was already thinking about me, even though I wasn’t there. I just felt like I was invisible, like no one wanted to talk to me because I didn’t fit in. I ended up staying inside the room and didn’t want to go back, so I just left early. Honestly, it’s hard to say what’s really going through my head—like, I’m always the one getting pushed aside or left out. It’s frustrating knowing that I’m missing out on things that could be fun for us all.

I guess I just… felt really uncomfortable when I realized they weren’t inviting me. It’s like no matter what I say, I’m just not enough to get in there. I keep thinking maybe they’re just annoyed that I’m not showing up or that I’m too annoying. So, I ended up telling them I had to leave because I was so tired and worried that I wouldn’t fit in. After that, I just stayed home and didn’t talk to anyone else for a few days. It’s hard to feel normal again after that kind of rejection. I wish they’d admit that they actually liked me or at least cared about me more than they seem to. It’s exhausting to keep pretending things aren’t wrong. I’ll probably stay away from them forever unless they change their mind.

Yeah, the dinner was… well, it felt kind of awkward. When someone said something, I just felt like maybe I wasn’t getting noticed or wanted. I kept thinking, “No one seems to care,” even though I tried to say something interesting or ask about stuff. Then, when they stopped talking, I stayed quiet, and I tried to hide my movements, hoping no one noticed I was bothering. It’s hard sometimes to connect with family unless I’m trying to impress everyone. I guess I feel pretty alone most days, especially at times like this where people seem less interested in me. It’s exhausting. I don’t know if there’s anything I can do differently to feel more connected.

Yeah, this week has been really tough so far. When I got the project assignment, I just felt like everyone was ignoring me because I didn’t seem important or needed help. I kept thinking, “Maybe they’re just not cut out for me,” so I ended up staying late and skipping meals to try and get it done. Even though I knew it wouldn’t make much difference, I still found myself laughing off any hints that I might be struggling. It’s exhausting to keep pushing through and feel like I’m not doing enough, especially when there’s always someone else taking care of things. Honestly, I don’t want anyone to know how exhausted I feel or how deeply hurt I’m feeling about the workload. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough. I guess I’m afraid to ask for help or admit that I’m struggling unless it’ll bring me closer to some kind of recognition or approval. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, maybe it’s just me not meeting their standards. But then again, I don’t really believe in that anyway—people just aren’t capable of being my friend. So, I just stick to trying harder than they ever would.

Well, since then, I’ve just stayed back and avoided the project altogether. I keep thinking, “What if I don’t make it through this? Everyone will see how much trouble I’m causing.” It’s exhausting and makes me feel like maybe I’m not cut out for this kind of responsibility. I guess I just don’t want to take on something that seems too daunting. Sometimes when I start talking about it, it feels even worse than the idea of doing nothing. It’s frustrating because I know I’m already struggling anyway, so being stuck isn’t helping. I guess I just feel like I’m not good enough for anything big.

I’ve been struggling lately because whenever I start thinking about going through with that job interview, I end up getting so anxious and afraid that I’m just plain showing everyone that I don’t want to do anything difficult. It’s like I’m always putting myself at risk, even though I know it wouldn’t help. I keep avoiding the whole thing because it seems too much to bear, and it makes me feel worse than if I didn’t have any options at all. Honestly, I just feel like maybe I’m failing somehow because no matter what I do, it doesn’t seem to make a difference. It’s like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop of self-doubt and dread. I guess I just don’t like being helpless anymore, and I’m scared that if I try harder, I’ll just embarrass myself or show off how inadequate I really am. It’s exhausting to keep pushing forward despite knowing it won’t change anything. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m not really thinking clearly—sometimes I just need a little more time to process everything. Please help me figure out why I’m stuck, okay? I appreciate you understanding me better. Thank you!

Yeah, so last night I went to that party and… um, honestly, I just felt really anxious. I kept thinking maybe I’ll sound funny or dumb, and then someone might notice and think I’m not really good enough. I guess I just wanted to fit in and make some kind of impression, but it ended up being worse than I imagined. I kept checking my phone to see if anyone seemed interested, but sometimes it’s like no one seems to care. It makes me feel scared that I’m not cut out for this type of social interaction. I guess it’s just easier to hide from those kinds of feelings when I know people are going to judge me.

Yeah, so, um, I got the offer for the art class last week, but then I realized it wasn’t really practical for me. Honestly, even though I’ve been trying to get some work done lately, I feel like I’m running out of steam anyway. I keep thinking, “What if I don’t finish that project? What if I just let myself fall behind?” And honestly, it just makes me feel worse than I already do. So, I decided not to go. I guess I’m just avoiding talking about it or maybe just pushing it off altogether because it feels easier not to deal with it. It’s like, no matter what I do, I just don’t seem to fit anywhere.

Yeah, the dinner was… well, just like last time. My parents always say something about how I need to fit in, and I just stayed quiet because I didn’t feel like talking about anything important. It felt like no matter what I said, it wasn’t really going to change things. After the dinner, I just went back home feeling frustrated and maybe even a little sad inside. I keep thinking that if I ever did something on my own, it’ll just come undone and I’ll fail again. It’s exhausting trying to figure things out when everyone seems to already know exactly where I’m headed. I guess I just don’t believe in myself anymore after being trapped in that role. It makes me want to drop out of life altogether.

Oh, well, this time it was like… I was just too much, you know? People’s eyes got bigger, and I started speaking really fast because I didn’t want to look silly or say something wrong. After that, I just went home and tried to figure everything out myself, but then I ended up feeling so ashamed and confused about what happened. It’s like when someone asks if I’m okay, and I just shrug and pretend I don’t care. I’ve been thinking about it all day, wondering why I keep getting caught up in things that aren’t mine. It makes me feel pretty useless sometimes. I guess I just can’t shake off the feeling that maybe I’m never going to be good enough for anyone. It’s like I’m constantly being judged and proven unworthy.

I’ve been dealing with this big project like it’s a game, and honestly, I’m just… stuck. Every time I get caught out, it makes me feel like maybe I don’t measure up, and no one there actually wants me around. I keep thinking, “If I mess up again, everyone will see I’m not cut out for this job.” So I’ve mostly avoided talking to anyone else lately—like most coworkers anyway. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like I’m losing something important in my life. I’m worried they might find out I’m struggling and judge me harshly. Honestly, it’s hard to explain how much I miss being part of our team, especially when I know I’m contributing to something crucial. I guess I just don’t feel like connecting anymore with people outside of work. It’s making me question whether I’m capable enough to succeed at what I’m trying to do. I’m sorry if this feels like a lot, but it’s been frustrating me for a long time.

Oh, so this morning, I was at the team meeting where we were supposed to work on three major projects really fast, but I kept getting pushed back and doubting myself. It felt like no matter how hard I tried, I just couldn’t keep up with everything. I ended up staying late because I didn’t want anyone to notice I wasn’t doing my job properly. Honestly, I feel like maybe if I had just said something less urgent, people wouldn’t have doubted my abilities. It’s exhausting—I just don’t want to go home after all this! I guess there’s a constant nagging in my mind that I’m not cut out for this kind of work. I hope they’ll see through me someday. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m capable and competent, but when people start怀疑我，我感觉自己已经不是什么人了。

I guess… well, at the reunion last week, it just didn’t feel right when everyone was talking about how my siblings were doing so much better than I was. I kept thinking maybe they’re just too smart or important for me to notice. So, I decided to stay home, staying behind and watching what happened on TV instead, even though I knew it wasn’t really going to change anything. It made me feel even worse about myself, like I wasn’t part of this big family anymore. I guess that whole feeling of being left out just makes me want to disappear. It’s frustrating because I’m scared I’ll lose someone special just because I don’t fit in. I don’t know why I keep avoiding things—maybe it’s because I’m afraid of making them uncomfortable or saying the wrong thing. It’s hard to connect with people if I always seem different or awkward. I wish there was some way for me to join in or share my story. But honestly, I don’t know—I just feel stuck in this empty space.

Well, yeah, it was really hard. When I started talking about how I felt like I wasn’t being noticed, they just went back to what they were doing. It made me feel even worse than usual, like maybe I’m not good enough in any way. And then after that, I just kind of decided to stay quiet and skip dinner because I didn’t want to start another argument or make things worse. Honestly, it’s frustrating knowing that I’m making them so angry and sad about nothing. I don’t know why I keep getting stuck in these situations where I’m always the one showing weakness. It’s like I’m invisible in my own head.

Yeah, it was really hard to stay involved at that family gathering. When everyone started talking about things, I just felt like I wasn’t even there, like nobody cared about what was happening inside me. I stayed quiet mostly because I wanted to keep away from anyone who might start a fight or complain. It’s frustrating because then I’d probably leave too quickly anyway. Honestly, I guess I’m just not very good at connecting with others in these situations. It’s like no matter how much I want to help them, they just don’t seem to notice or care about me. It’s exhausting to feel so invisible when you’re trying to show interest and support. I wish I could talk to someone and say something about what’s bothering me, but I’m afraid it’ll just make things worse. It’s like I’m stuck in this hole where I’m constantly being pushed aside.

Hey, therapist. I just went to this dinner with some friends, and I noticed that I wasn’t really contributing much, even though everyone seemed interested in talking. It’s like no one actually cared about what I had to say. I kept thinking maybe they’re bored or that I’m boring them out of interest. After coming back home, I just sat there all night, replaying what happened and wondering why I didn’t get involved more. It makes me feel really sad and rejected. I don’t know if I can keep going through this without getting lonely or feeling like I’m not good enough. Please let me know if I need to talk about these feelings further. I appreciate you thinking deeper about what I’m doing. I just want to feel better before things get worse. Thanks!

Well, I guess I was really nervous at first. When I saw the calendar for the workshop, I immediately started thinking about all the things that could go wrong—my presentation getting too messy, maybe making a mistake that people won’t notice. Then, I kept replaying those parts of what happened over and over in my head, like “Oh no, I’ll just mess up.” It felt pretty overwhelming, kind of like I’m constantly failing at something even though I know it shouldn’t be that way. I ended up declining the invite because honestly, I didn’t want to deal with all that stress or embarrassment. Plus, I just felt so ashamed of myself after that, wondering why I couldn’t handle these kinds of things better. It’s frustrating because sometimes we need to face our fears head-on, but sometimes we just don’t want to admit they’re there.

Well, during that gathering, I felt really overwhelmed. I was trying to say something different, but when someone said what I didn’t agree with, I just ended up agreeing anyway. It made me feel like I wasn’t good enough or important enough to speak up. Then later, I kept thinking about how easy it was if I had actually spoken up, but it just made me feel even worse about myself. Honestly, I guess it’s always easier to stay quiet than to deal with things that aren’t mine. I don’t know why I’m stuck in this loop of needing to fit in but failing every time. It’s frustrating because I want to be strong and confident, but sometimes I just can’t quite break free from these small pressures. I’ll admit that feeling helpless and embarrassed afterward. It makes me question if I’m really capable of being my own person.

Yeah, lately, when I’m trying to keep up with them, I just feel like something’s going wrong. They usually don’t respond right away because it’s hard for me to say anything important, and then I end up feeling like I’m losing them or that they’re bored out of their minds. It’s exhausting and makes me question if I’m really making a difference. Sometimes I just try to pretend everything’s okay, but it just doesn’t work out the way I want. I guess I’m scared that if I show weakness or express myself, they’ll just leave me. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but I just can’t stop worrying about keeping up. I feel like I’m failing at this relationship.

Well, the recent situation when my friend didn’t invite me to the party made me feel really sad. I kept thinking maybe they’re just looking for some quiet space or something, like they don’t want me around because I’m annoying them or something. I ended up isolating myself too much, staying away from them even though I felt uncomfortable. It’s frustrating because I’ve tried to reach out earlier but just didn’t get the response I wanted. Honestly, it makes me question if I’m truly valued or loved anywhere. I guess this experience reminds me that sometimes we miss our own importance in a group setting. It’s exhausting to deal with things like this. I’ll probably keep trying harder to make friends later, but right now, I’m just stuck in this state of being left behind.

Yeah, um, last week I got a notice about the utilities, and I just… It felt like no matter how hard I tried, I couldn’t pay them this month. I kept thinking, “Why bother? Everyone else seems to handle it better,” and then I just ended up avoiding talking about it. Honestly, I don’t want anyone to know about it; it just makes me feel even worse. I guess I’m scared that if I tell them, they’ll laugh at me or think less of me. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m capable of dealing with this kind of thing, but somehow I just don’t seem to connect with it. I wish there was some way to make things better, but it feels like nothing’s working out right. I’m sorry if I’m causing any problems, but I don’t want anyone to see how stuck I am.

Yeah, last week was really rough. I got this bill notice right after work, and honestly, I just froze. I didn’t want to look at it because I figured if people saw how helpless I was, maybe they’d think less of me. I’ve been avoiding talking to my landlord because I’m scared he’ll find out about the bills. It feels like everyone’s already thinking about how much trouble I’m getting into, even though I don’t really feel bad about it. I guess I’m just stuck in a cycle where I don’t want to face anything challenging or embarrassing myself. It’s hard to talk to friends or family because I’m afraid they’ll say something negative about me. I wish I could get some help or explain things better, but it feels like I’m trapped in my own mind. It’s frustrating and scary all at once.

Yeah, this new project feels like just another big weight on my back. I keep thinking, “What if I screw something up? Everyone will realize how stupid I really am.” I’m always afraid that if I don’t get everything done perfectly, people will notice how badly I need help. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know why I even bother trying. Sometimes I just end up skipping classes because I’m scared of being seen as a failure. It’s hard to stay focused and productive when I’m constantly worrying about what they’ll say or think about me. I guess I just feel like maybe there’s no point in trying anymore—I’ve tried too many times already. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle of perfectionism that never really works out for me.

Yeah, the meeting went pretty slow. I kept thinking that everyone was already well-versed in the project, so why didn’t I come up with something? It’s like I’m invisible when I’m around them, and then I feel like I’m just another part of what they’re missing. Afterward, I just felt really alone and kind of overwhelmed, like maybe I don’t belong anywhere. I guess I’m afraid if I open up, they’ll think less of me or leave me behind. It’s hard to connect with anyone else unless I’m actually contributing something meaningful. I’ve tried to push harder lately, but nothing seems to stick. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of being judged and not doing anything right. I feel like my own insecurities are starting to take over, and I’m scared that if I start speaking up, I’ll just embarrass myself even further. I guess I just want to make sure I’m good enough before I lose everything.

I’ve been avoiding checking my bank notice because… well, I just feel like I’m going to mess up something important. It’s like no matter what I try, it always fails, and then I’m left feeling like I’m really no good at anything. I keep thinking maybe everyone else is handling their money just fine, so I don’t want to look foolish or prove that I’m incompetent. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m just hoping that if I wait another day, things will improve somehow. But instead, I end up being caught up in something even worse than staying out all night. I guess I’m scared of getting too close to problems and making them harder to fix. It’s frustrating because it makes me feel like nothing matters anymore. So, I’m just okay with putting myself through this alone tonight. It’s exhausting but also comforting sometimes.

Well, lately, I’ve just gotten this notice about some bank fees that seem really big and overwhelming. It’s like nothing else comes along that’s as stressful as dealing with money right now. I feel so overwhelmed because even though I know I’m supposed to pay them, I’m afraid I’ll mess things up or make them worse than they already are. So, I’m staying home and trying to ignore it, but then I start feeling anxious again, kind of like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop where I don’t want to move forward or say anything. It makes me feel like maybe I’m not good enough or that no one cares about me at all. I guess I just need to get away from these worries and find somewhere to relax, but it’s hard when I’m already stressed and unsure of what to do. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I’m always worrying and then failing. I hope I can stop pretending I’m okay or convincing anyone that I’m capable. But honestly, I just don’t feel ready to face anything like this alone.

Yeah, lately, when I’m around this new person, it’s like… every time I start talking, I just freeze up, and then my mind goes blank again. I keep thinking maybe they’re losing interest because I’ve already shown how uncomfortable I am. Or maybe they’re just starting to realize how different I really am. So, I tend to just shut down, or ask them all sorts of questions that make me feel even more out of place. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know what to do. Sometimes I just want to disappear, but I worry that if I’m too withdrawn, they’ll see I’m not worth keeping. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never going to make things better between us.

Oh, hey, Mike. I was thinking about this project right after our meeting, and honestly, I didn’t really notice anyone saying much because I kept thinking maybe I’m just going to mess up. But then I saw some of them nodding along anyway, so I just copied what they said, even though I wasn’t really sure what I wanted to say. It feels like no matter how hard I try, I’m never quite good enough. I guess I feel like if I really tried, maybe I’d fail and everyone would think less of me. It’s hard to talk myself out of it because I want to make things better for everyone, but sometimes it just feels too overwhelming. I appreciate you taking me on, but it’s tough to stay focused there. I guess I just don’t know how to deal with these kinds of pressures. I’m sorry if I’m making things worse for you. I need some help here. Please tell me what I should do differently next time. I just want to feel better. Thank you!

Yeah, the project meeting was really tough for me. When I got the assignment, I started to feel like something was going to go wrong, like I was putting all my skills on the line. Honestly, I just kept running around and didn’t stop until I was stuck in another meeting trying to explain what I had done wrong. It’s exhausting because I’m afraid people might notice how badly I’m failing. Sometimes I just shut down completely, pretending things aren’t happening so they won’t catch me. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never quite good enough. I’ve been thinking a lot lately that maybe there’s something wrong with me, and I don’t want anyone to see how much of a failure I’ve become. It’s exhausting to keep pushing through, even though I know I’m making mistakes. I guess I just need someone to tell me what’s really happening and help me understand where I’m going wrong. It’s frustrating knowing that sometimes, even when I’m struggling, people still think I’m capable. I’m sorry if this makes me seem grumpy or unprofessional, but I just feel scared to talk. I don’t know why I’m always doing this, and it’s taking me longer than I’d like to admit. Please let me know what’s going on, okay? I need some answers.

Well, lately, when I went out with this friend, it felt almost like everyone else was already bored with me. We talked about something simple and boring, and then suddenly, they stopped talking and seemed to feel pretty uncomfortable. I kept apologizing and pretending everything was okay, but honestly, I was feeling really overwhelmed and maybe a little bit depressed. I guess I’m afraid they’ll say I’m stupid again, and that makes me feel even worse about myself. So, I ended up just staying away from them after that. It’s hard to believe that I’m still having this kind of reaction sometimes. I hope they learn better next time. I just don’t want to go through all that again.

Well, last week was really rough when I got the notice about the bill and the money on hold. I felt this overwhelming weight in my chest because it seemed like everyone was just thinking the same thing—I’m failing. I kept thinking maybe they’re judging me or seeing me as something I can’t handle. It’s hard to talk to people or even look at things; I feel so helpless and alone. Honestly, I don’t want to face anyone and don’t know what to say. It’s like nothing makes sense anymore. I guess I just don’t believe in myself, and I’m afraid if I show weakness, they’ll see me as a failure. It’s exhausting to stay hidden from friends, and then I feel completely alone. I hope they’ll stop thinking I’m such a failure and let me know I’m okay. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of self-doubt and isolation.

Yeah, last week, he mentioned that I didn’t seem to care much anymore about how we were spending our time together. It made me feel like maybe I’m just not really interested in him at all. I kept thinking, “Why don’t I need to be around him? He’ll probably leave if he sees I’m being too needy.” So, I ended up making plans to go out more often, even though he wanted some alone time. It just felt harder than it already did because I was scared of losing him or making things worse. Honestly, I guess it’s hard to keep pushing myself to connect when I know I’m going to disappoint him. It’s like I’m always putting on a show, but then it just leaves me feeling more confused and disappointed. I’m sorry if this comes off as unhelpful or disrespectful—it’s just hard for me to explain exactly why I’m doing this. I appreciate you stepping into the role of therapist, but I’m afraid I’m not really getting through to you. Please let me know if there’s anything I could change. I’d love to talk through this more, but I’m scared of making things worse. I’m scared that if I tell him I’m struggling, he’ll decide I’m not cut out for him. It’s like I’m afraid he’ll think less of me if I reveal my feelings. So, thank you so much for taking the time to talk to me this week. I really appreciate you being here. I hope you understand how frustrating it is for me to feel like I’m never good enough.

Oh, well, lately, whenever I’m supposed to tackle something big like that at work, I just feel like I’m always screwing things up. It’s like no matter what I try, I keep falling apart and people just assume I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff. Plus, there’s this creeping sense of dread every time I think about doing anything wrong because I’m worried that if I fail, they’ll all go away. So, I’ve been putting off the whole thing because I don’t want to risk making things worse or losing face. It’s hard to say, though—sometimes I just feel really overwhelmed and sick inside. I guess I’m scared that if I show any signs of trying, it’ll just prove how incompetent I am.

Yeah, this past week was really tough. When I got the notice of overdue payments, I just felt like all the pieces were falling apart. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t fix it quickly enough? No one else seems to know what’s going on either.” So, instead of trying to address it, I just ignored them and tried to keep pretending everything’s okay, even though deep down I knew it wasn’t. It’s frustrating because I want to make things better, but I’m just stuck in a loop where nothing changes. I guess I’m scared of making mistakes or admitting my flaws, which makes me feel even worse. It’s like I’m always questioning why I can’t handle these things, especially when they’re happening so frequently.

I don’t know, therapist. Yesterday at the family gathering, something happened where everyone seemed to be looking elsewhere when I tried to speak up. It felt like no matter what I said, it didn’t make any difference, and then they just ignored me completely. I kept thinking, “They’re all so cold and unfriendly,” and it made me feel really bad and… like maybe I’m not good enough. After that, I just shut down and stayed quiet for most of the day, and then started feeling even worse. I guess it’s hard to connect with people unless I seem interesting or useful somehow. It makes me feel like I’m not really part of whatever they want to build around us. I’m scared I’ll never get back to them or figure out how to change things for better. It’s exhausting to deal with this kind of constant rejection. I just wish I could stop feeling so guilty about being invisible. Please, let me go through this with you, okay? I need you to see how deeply hurt and rejected I am.

I guess… Well, last week, I was really stressing about the project because everyone was looking at me like I wasn’t good enough. I kept thinking, “If I mess up, people will see I’m not cut out for this job.” So, I started working late every night and barely slept, even though I knew it wouldn’t make a difference. It’s hard to talk to my coworkers because I’m afraid they’ll find out I’m struggling or maybe think less of me. I felt so anxious and scared, like I’m always failing, and no matter what I do, it just won’t go well. It’s frustrating because I want to prove myself, but I’m scared of making mistakes that could ruin everything. I guess I’m scared that if I show any weakness, people will see how inadequate I am. It’s exhausting and makes me question why I choose to take on such a big responsibility. I need someone to listen and understand me better, but I’m too scared to speak up. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of fear and uncertainty.

Well, since then, I’ve just been carrying this load like it’s a joke. I keep pushing harder, skipping lunch because every time I feel like maybe something won’t go right, I just shut down completely. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know what to say to anyone. I’m scared that no one will understand how much I struggle. It’s like I’m invisible to people around me, and I’m just stuck here trying to figure out how to fix things instead of finding a friend to share some of these problems with. It’s really overwhelming. I guess I just want to disappear somewhere else, but I’m afraid that’s where I’ll end up alone again. It feels like no matter how hard I try, I’m just going to fall apart. I don’t know why I’m stuck here, but sometimes it feels like nothing makes any difference anymore.

Oh, hey therapist. So this past week was really tough, huh? When I tried to help out with that big project, I just felt like no one was paying attention to what I was doing. I kept thinking maybe they’re too busy or that I’m not cut out for this kind of work. It’s hard to stop trying because I want to prove to them that I’m capable and important, but then I end up feeling worse off afterwards. I guess I just don’t feel like part of the team anymore, so I’m avoiding social gatherings whenever possible. It’s like, if I’m not there, I’ll never get noticed or understood again. I don’t know why I keep falling into this cycle. I appreciate you reminding me sometimes though, just to remind me that I still exist. But honestly, it feels like I’m always failing.

Yeah, so last night, I was at that group meeting where everyone was talking about their projects and stuff. It felt like I was just there, kind of floating around instead of participating. I kept thinking, “What if no one notices what I’m saying? Or maybe it’s just because I’m not really interested.” I ended up leaving earlier than planned because I wanted to make sure I didn’t miss out on something important. Sometimes when I feel like I’m missing out, I just shut down and don’t want to talk anymore. It’s hard to explain why I’m avoiding things—I just feel invisible. It makes me wonder if I’m being weak or not good enough. I guess there’s just something about being part of something that keeps me from feeling connected or included.

Hey therapist, I’ve been feeling really stuck lately. When something happens at work that’s causing stress—like losing another job—I find myself just shutting down and trying to pretend everything’s okay. It’s like I’m always falling apart, and instead of getting help, I end up doing more of it on my own. It’s exhausting, and I don’t want to face anything bigger because I worry it’ll make me worse. Sometimes, I just wish there was some magic button that lets me fix this mess. I guess I’m just afraid of what might happen if I let myself get lost in the chaos. It feels like I’m trapped in a never-ending loop of failure. Please, let me talk about these feelings in more detail so I understand better. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out where I’m going wrong.

Yeah, it’s like whenever someone talks about their kids’ stuff or mentions their own interests, I just feel this whole thing goes away. It’s like they’re ignoring me even though I’m there. Honestly, it makes me feel like I’m invisible, and then I start thinking maybe they don’t really care about me either. I’ve tried to tell my friends about my work, but nothing seems to stick. After that, I just ended up staying quiet because it felt easier not to talk. It’s like no matter how much I try to connect, it never feels right. I guess I just don’t believe in the power of being seen anymore. It’s exhausting to keep feeling so helpless when everything feels like it’s happening to me. I wish I could change anything, but honestly, I don’t know what to say. It’s like no matter what I do, it doesn’t feel enough. I’ll probably go back home later, but right now, I just feel drained and hopeless.

Yeah, this evening was really hard for me. When I started talking, someone suddenly came around and said something, interrupting me, which made me feel like I wasn’t part of the conversation. Then, I kept trying to explain things myself because I wanted them to understand I was there, but it always felt like they were judging me instead. I ended up sending follow-ups afterward because I didn’t want anyone to think I wasnn’t interested anymore. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like maybe I’m not enough or interesting enough. I guess whenever I see people around, I just want to disappear and pretend everything’s okay, but it’s easier than having to face the pain of not fitting in. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to stop thinking these negative thoughts. I wish I could talk to more people later and make more friends. It’s like no matter what I do, I just seem to fall apart.

Oh, hey there! So, last night was super awkward at the party because I kept trying to join in, but it felt like no one wanted to talk to me. I guess when I saw everyone turn around or leave, I just felt invisible, kind of like I’m the reason everyone else is here. It makes me want to run away early or pretend nothing happened. Honestly, I don’t know what to say anymore. Sometimes I wonder why I always end up feeling so useless or left out. It’s exhausting trying to make friends and keep going unnoticed. Do you think maybe it’s because I’m always missing something important? Or am I just carrying on anyway because it’s easier than dealing with the pain? I’d love for anyone to understand how much I’m struggling. Thanks for asking!

Yeah, so lately, when they start looking at the big plan, I feel like I’m always seeing that someone else is taking care of everything. It makes me feel like maybe I’ll mess things up or let them down again. Honestly, I’ve been trying to stay quiet but then I just end up doing too much later and it takes longer than I expected. Sometimes I even skip meetings because I’m afraid I’ll just fall asleep or forget something important. It’s exhausting and makes me feel really helpless. I guess I just don’t want to take on this responsibility because it feels like it’s going to get worse.

Oh, yeah, that was really tough. When they started talking about what we could do together, I felt like I was just not getting noticed at all. I kept thinking, “They’re probably too busy thinking about something else that doesn’t matter to me.” After that, I just decided to stay quiet because I didn’t want to start a fight or say anything wrong. It made me feel worse than I already did being ignored in the first place. I guess I just don’t belong there anymore. It makes me feel so bad that even though I’m tired of arguing, I still end up feeling this way. I wish I could tell them I’m sorry, but it feels safer not to talk about it. I appreciate any chance you give me to show that I’m not completely useless, but honestly, I’m scared of what others might think. I’ll probably head home early anyway, hoping nobody notices me again.

Yeah, so recently, I got this big project at work with all the pressure just hanging over me. I keep thinking, “I’ll screw this up like every other time.” Then I just cancel it because it seems easier than trying again. And honestly, even when I ask for help, people just look at me and say I’m not cut out for the job. It’s like I’m invisible, and no one sees what’s really happening inside me. I feel so anxious and kind of scared to face anything challenging. It’s like when I’m afraid of falling down in front of everyone—like I’m already failing anyway. So, I’ve been avoiding the whole thing because it’s too overwhelming and makes me want to collapse. It feels like no matter what I do, I’m never good enough, and I don’t know why I keep getting stuck in this cycle of perfectionism. I guess I just feel trapped in this senseless cycle of being a failure.

I’ve been feeling really overwhelmed lately because I’m so close to hitting the deadline for that big project. When someone says they’re doing better than others, I start doubting myself. I always stay late at work to make sure everything is perfect, even if it means missing sleep or food. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m still failing, and that makes me feel ashamed and helpless. Honestly, I don’t want anyone to know how I’m feeling—like I’m not cut out for this kind of responsibility. I keep thinking maybe they just find another person who’s more capable and won’t need me anymore. It’s exhausting and leaves me feeling scared and hopeless. I guess I just don’t trust myself to get through this. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I feel more pressure than ever to succeed.

I don’t know, therapist. I’ve been feeling really stuck lately. When I get a new project at work, I just start feeling like I’m going to mess everything up. It’s like no matter what I try, I’ll just end up looking silly or not fit in. I’m afraid of being judged or seen as someone who can’t handle the pressure. Sometimes I just want to put off doing anything because it’s easier to avoid facing the consequences than deal with the doubts and insecurities. I guess I just feel so helpless right now. It’s exhausting to keep pushing through though, because even then, I’m starting to doubt myself. I don’t want anyone to see me as a failure, especially when they’re already judging me. I’m sorry if this feels like a real person responding to these feelings. Please let me know if there’s anything I can do to help. I’d appreciate any advice you can give me. I’m just hoping we can figure out something better.

Yeah, the meeting was really tough for me. I just felt like I had to prove myself again, even though I’m already pretty exhausted from all this work. I kept thinking maybe if I said yes, people would realize I’m not cut out for this kind of pressure. After that, I ended up spending all day just trying to keep up, even when it wasn’t helping. It’s exhausting to feel so helpless and then having to deal with more stress later. I guess I just don’t like being seen as someone who needs help anymore. It’s hard to focus on anything else once I’ve pushed myself too far.
